decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Since geometry changes are hitting legality limits and recent improvements have stalled, the strategy shifts to material tuning. The heat spreader conductivity is currently at its upper bound (500.0), so we will increase the base plate conductivity to improve downstream heat extraction toward the side boundaries.

changes.reason: Doubling the base plate conductivity within the allowed 2.0x ratio enhances lateral heat spreading in the lower domain, facilitating better heat removal to the Dirichlet boundaries on the left and right.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip peak temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
Improved thermal gradient across the base plate
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck remains strictly within the spreader or chip interface rather than the base.
Increasing base conductivity too aggressively in future steps could mask other geometric inefficiencies.
